title: Floor live user count at 1 on room join and remove 'online' text

requirements.text: In ChatArea.tsx, when rendering the live user count inside a chatroom, floor the displayed value at a minimum of 1 whenever the local user is present in the room. This ensures the count never shows 0 while the user is viewing the room.

quotes: The count should never be 0 because that wouldn't make sense
floor it at 1 (since you are there)

acceptanceCriteria: The live user count displayed in ChatArea never shows 0 while the user is inside a room
If the backend returns a count >= 1, that real count is shown without modification
If the backend returns 0 (e.g., heartbeat not yet responded), the UI displays 1 instead

requirements.text: In ChatroomPage.tsx (or useHeartbeat.ts), fire an immediate heartbeat call to the backend as soon as the user joins/enters a room, without waiting for the first 45-second interval tick. This causes the real backend count to sync quickly after join.

quotes: Immediate heartbeat on join so the real count syncs quickly from the backend

acceptanceCriteria: A heartbeat is sent to the backend immediately on room entry (not waiting for the 45-second interval)
The periodic 45-second heartbeat continues to run as before
The live user count updates to the real backend value as soon as the immediate heartbeat response arrives

requirements.text: Remove the word 'online' (or any 'online' label/text) from the live user count display in ChatArea.tsx. The count should display without an 'online' suffix or prefix.
acceptanceCriteria: The text 'online' does not appear anywhere in the live user count display inside a chatroom
The count number itself is still displayed

constraints: Do not add caller checks to any backend functions if the backend is modified
Do not modify files listed under frontend.immutablePaths
nonGoals: Optimistic update approach for user count (explicitly rejected)
Any changes to the 45-second heartbeat interval
